steps.title: Identify the rhetorical move in the underlined sentence
steps.content: The first sentence frames Nixon primarily through the Watergate scandal, creating a single-dimensional perception. The underlined sentence begins with "But," signaling a direct contrast. It then presents Nixon's achievements and other aspects of his legacy — information that complicates and pushes back against the opening's narrow portrayal.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It compares the achievements of two historical figures.': The underlined sentence discusses only Nixon; no second historical figure is introduced or compared. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It offers information suggesting that a particular perception of a historical figure is an oversimplification.': The sentence counters the Watergate-only view of Nixon by presenting evidence of his broader legacy, showing that perception is incomplete. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It provides evidence supporting a claim about a historical figure and that figure's associates.': The underlined sentence focuses on Nixon himself; his associates are not discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It explains why a historical figure's accomplishments are often disregarded by historians.': The sentence highlights Nixon's accomplishments but does not explain why historians disregard them. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
